message:
fix(core,alloc,ondisk): replace lossy as-casts with checked try_from conversions

Replace silent `as u32`/`as u16` truncation in critical filesystem paths
with `u32::try_from()` + proper error handling. Prevents potential data
corruption on large ext4 images where block numbers, inode numbers, or
group counts could silently overflow 32-bit fields.

Key fixes:
- ext4_write: logical block from file offset now validated via try_from
- ext4_setattr: truncation logical end validated
- groups_count: capped at u32::MAX instead of silent truncation
- absolute_to_group_block: group number validated
- Directory entries: inode numbers validated before u32 conversion
- init_dir_block: parent/child inode validated
- Also fixes const u32::from issue (not const-stable)

bd-3gck, bd-2v0n

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
